Title: Abscisic acid promotes jasmonic acid biosynthesis via a SAPK10-bZIP72-AOC pathway to synergistically inhibit seed germination in rice

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of COI1 reduces ABA sensitivity during seed germination and seedling growth, indicating JA signaling contributes to ABA-imposed inhibition.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Inhibits seed germination and contributes to stress and defense signaling.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant phenotype analysis under ABA treatment; seed germination and seedling growth assays
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous ABA treatment during seed germination
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: COI1 was used as JA signaling evidence rather than a primary mechanistic node of the SAPK10-bZIP72-AOC pathway.
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: JA-Ile signaling components (not experimentally tested in this paper)
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. coi1 mutants showed reduced sensitivity to ABA during germination. 2. This supports that JA signaling acts downstream of ABA-promoted JA accumulation. 3. COI1 was not the central mechanistic focus but provided functional pathway evidence.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to modulate hormone crosstalk and germination sensitivity, but likely with broad pleiotropic effects on JA responses.

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Midstream to committed JA-biosynthetic step leading to 12-OPDA formation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Allene oxide derived from 13-hydroperoxy linolenic acid via AOS
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes unstable allene oxide intermediate to form 12-oxophytodienoic acid, a key precursor of jasmonic acid.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor of the target product jasmonic acid
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control/rate-limiting JA-biosynthetic gene in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: AOC activation increases JA accumulation and enhances ABA-mediated inhibition of seed germination; AOC mutant cpm2 shows reduced JA and ABA hyposensitivity.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Inhibits seed germination and participates in stress/defense responses.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: AOC transcript is induced by bZIP72 and ABA in germinating seeds
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in germinating embryos under ABA and in OxbZIP72 lines; downregulated in crbzip72/crtrab1
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter analyzed; G-box-containing promoter bound by bZIP72
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant phenotype analysis (cpm2); qRT-PCR; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR; dual-luciferase assay; JA quantification
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous ABA treatment; ibuprofen inhibition of JA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altered JA-dependent growth inhibition during germination and post-germination growth
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Prior phosphoproteomic study cited for bZIP72 phosphorylation, but no accession reported in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AOC is treated as a master regulator/key node of JA biosynthesis in this study and is directly activated by phosphorylated bZIP72.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AOC is a direct transcriptional target of phosphorylated bZIP72 through a G-box in its promoter. 2. ABA enhances bZIP72 binding and activation of AOC. 3. The aoc mutant cpm2 has lower JA content and reduced ABA sensitivity during germination, supporting AOC as a key node in ABA-JA crosstalk.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating AOC could tune JA accumulation and germination behavior, but may affect broad JA-dependent stress and growth traits.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression of SAPK10 elevates JA accumulation and causes hypersensitivity to ABA, leading to stronger inhibition of seed germination and seedling growth.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key ABA-signaling upstream activator linking ABA to JA biosynthesis through phosphorylation of bZIP72.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Inhibits seed germination and participates in stress/defense responses.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: JA biosynthesis via transcriptional activation of AOC through phosphorylated bZIP72
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Phosphorylates and stabilizes bZIP72, thereby promoting AOC transcription and JA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Overlapped with bZIP72 according to supporting data; exact compartment not clearly stated
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Relatively high in developing seeds; induced by exogenous ABA during seed germination
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Relatively high in developing seeds; induced by ABA during germination
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression and CRISPR knockout; autophosphorylation assay; phospho-mutant analysis; Y2H; pull-down; Co-IP; in vitro kinase assay; JA quantification; germination assays
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous ABA treatment; ibuprofen treatment during germination
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Retarded seed germination and seedling growth when overexpressed
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Autophosphorylation at Ser177 is required for kinase activity but not for self-binding or binding to bZIP72; knockout phenotype likely masked by redundancy with SAPK8/9 and others.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SAPK10 is autophosphorylated mainly at Ser177, and ABA enhances its phosphorylation. 2. SAPK10 interacts with and phosphorylates bZIP72, with Ser177 required for kinase activity. 3. SAPK10 overexpression increases ABA sensitivity and JA accumulation, while the S177A version loses this effect. 4. SAPK10 functions as the upstream ABA-responsive kinase in the SAPK10-bZIP72-AOC pathway.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to manipulate ABA-JA crosstalk and germination vigor, but overactivation causes delayed germination and growth penalty.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: bZIP72 promotes JA accumulation and ABA hypersensitivity during seed germination by directly activating AOC transcription after SAPK10-mediated phosphorylation.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Direct activator of AOC and major transcriptional switch connecting ABA signaling to JA biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Inhibits seed germination and participates in stress/defense responses.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: JA biosynthesis; also potentially GA homeostasis
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly activates AOC transcription by binding the promoter G-box; upregulates multiple JA biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation enhanced by phosphorylation-dependent protein stabilization and DNA binding
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions redundantly with TRAB1 in ABA signaling
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: SAPK10; TRAB1 (functional redundancy, not direct interaction shown)
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Overlapped with SAPK10 according to supporting data; exact compartment not clearly stated
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Relatively high in developing seeds; induced by exogenous ABA during seed germination
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Relatively high in developing seeds; induced by ABA during germination
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Maize ubiquitin promoter for overexpression; 35S effector in transient assay
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression and CRISPR mutant/double mutant analyses; Y2H; pull-down; Co-IP; in vitro phosphorylation assay; cell-free degradation assay; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR; dual-luciferase assay; JA quantification
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous ABA treatment; ibuprofen treatment during germination
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression retards germination and seedling growth
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Prior phosphoproteomic study identified Ser71 ABA-induced phosphorylation; accession not reported here
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Ser71 is the major SAPK10-dependent phosphosite; phosphorylation stabilizes bZIP72 against 26S proteasome degradation and enhances AOC promoter binding. bZIP72 may also bind GA20ox2 promoter, suggesting broader hormone crosstalk.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. bZIP72 physically interacts with SAPK10 and is phosphorylated mainly at Ser71. 2. Phosphorylation stabilizes bZIP72 and increases its DNA-binding ability. 3. bZIP72 directly binds the G-box in the AOC promoter and activates AOC transcription, especially under ABA. 4. Overexpression causes ABA hypersensitivity and higher JA, whereas the S71A mutant loses this activity. 5. bZIP72 acts redundantly with TRAB1 in ABA signaling during seed germination.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising regulator for engineering hormone crosstalk and germination behavior, but constitutive activation may strongly penalize germination and early growth.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: TRAB1 functions redundantly with bZIP72 in ABA signaling; only the bzip72/trab1 double mutant reduces ABA sensitivity and JA-biosynthetic gene expression.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Redundant positive regulator supporting ABA-mediated JA biosynthesis and germination inhibition.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Inhibits seed germination and participates in stress/defense responses.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Supports ABA-linked regulation of JA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: AOC and other JA biosynthetic genes (inferred from double-mutant expression changes)
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Positive redundant contribution to expression of JA biosynthetic genes in the ABA pathway
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: ABA signaling; likely SAPK10 as reported previously, but not mechanistically dissected here
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: TRAB1 transcription is upregulated in bzip72 mutants
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: TRAB1 is transcriptionally upregulated when bZIP72 is mutated
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR single and double mutant phenotyping; qRT-PCR of JA pathway genes; JA quantification in double mutant
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Single mutant shows little phenotype due to redundancy
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TRAB1 was not mechanistically characterized in this paper beyond redundancy with bZIP72; included because double-mutant analysis in Results establishes it as a core experimental gene.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TRAB1 alone shows little germination phenotype, but bZIP72/TRAB1 double knockout reduces ABA sensitivity. 2. The double mutant has lower JA content and reduced expression of JA biosynthesis genes including AOC. 3. TRAB1 therefore functions redundantly with bZIP72 in ABA-JA crosstalk during seed germination.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential combinatorial target with bZIP72 for editing ABA sensitivity, though redundancy means single-gene manipulation may be insufficient.
